mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS path transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation support, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when rate cuts occur during acute recession scares that overwhelm discount-rate relief.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising policy rates, or if the sign of the rolling 3-year rank correlation is persistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE above its recent trend identifies weakening labor income and recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when unemployment rises because it is driven by temporary labor-supply re-entry rather than demand deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative average 20-day returns, or if SPX is positive in more than 60% of UNRATE-surge windows over the evaluation sample.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS and RSAFS both accelerate, the economy is simultaneously adding payroll income and retail spending power, which supports near-term earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect should weaken if one series rises while the other stalls.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS acceleration state does not outperform each single-series state on 5-day forward returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A falling INDPRO indicates weaker industrial output and softer real activity, which should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because cyclical earnings expectations are revised down. The signal fails when industrial production falls only because of temporary inventory adjustment without broader demand loss.
failure_condition: Reject if negative 1-month changes in INDPRO are not associated with below-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the rolling sign of the relationship flips positive for extended periods.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation rises above the policy rate, real rates turn more restrictive and equity valuation multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if inflation is rising but remains anchored enough that discount-rate pressure does not tighten in real terms.
failure_condition: Reject if CPI above policy does not precede weaker 60-day SPX performance, or if the CPI-minus-FEDFUNDS spread has no incremental explanatory power beyond either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals collapsing housing demand and tighter cyclical financing conditions, which should lead S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days as investors price slower aggregate demand. The signal fails when housing weakness is offset by easier monetary policy or a rebound in other demand indicators.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing declines do not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is not below 40% in the out-of-sample triggered sample.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS print during a rising UNRATE regime marks genuine labor-market deterioration rather than statistical noise, so S&P 500 should deliver negative 5-day returns. The effect fails when payroll softness is isolated and unemployment remains stable.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 5-day SPX returns are not negative in most joint-signal instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery, concurrent gains in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicate real output and consumer demand are both reaccelerating, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when GDP growth is inventory-driven and retail sales do not confirm final demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and retail-sales acceleration does not predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal loses significance after accounting for each series separately.
